title: Torque impact mitigator for power tong
abstract: A torque impact mitigator including a housing assembly having a hydraulic cylinder. A piston is disposed within the hydraulic cylinder. A piston rod is mounted at a first end to the piston and having a second end extending out from the compression end. A compression spring is disposed between the piston and an end of the cylinder. A rod clevis is secured to the second end of the piston rod to compress the compression spring into a compressed state when the rod clevis is moved away from the cylinder and to release the compression spring into a relaxed state when the rod clevis is moved towards the cylinder. A plug is disposed within an upper end of the compression spring and having a bore extending therethrough to receive the piston rod. One or more bores are disposed through the piston to allow passage of hydraulic fluid into the hydraulic cylinder.
claims:
Claims In the claims: 1. A torque impact mitigator (10), comprising: a housing assembly (20) including a hydraulic cylinder (26) having a compression end (26a) and a rebound end (26b); a piston (44) disposed within the hydraulic cylinder (26) so as to reciprocate back and forth; a piston rod (28) disposed within the hydraulic cylinder (26) and mounted at a first end to the piston and having a second end extending out from the compression end; a compression spring (60) disposed between the piston (44) and the compression end (22a) of the hydraulic cylinder (26); a rod clevis (90) secured to the second end of the piston rod (28) to compress the compression spring (60) into a compressed state when the rod clevis is moved away from the hydraulic cylinder and to release the compression spring into a relaxed state when the rod clevis is moved towards the hydraulic cylinder; a plug (62) is disposed within an upper end (60a) of the compression spring and having a bore (64) extending therethrough to receive the piston rod; and one or more bores (56) disposed through the piston to allow the passage of hydraulic fluid into the compression end of the hydraulic cylinder when the compression spring is in the compressed state, and to allow the passage of hydraulic fluid into the rebound end of the hydraulic cylinder when the compression spring is in the relaxed state. 2. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 1 wherein the housing assembly (20) includes first and second end caps (22, 24), wherein the compression end (26a) of the hydraulic cylinder (26) is secured to the first end cap and the rebound end (26b) of the hydraulic cylinder is secured to the second end cap. 3. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 1 wherein the compression spring (60) is disposed about the piston rod (28) and against the piston (44) and against the plug (62). 4. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 3 wherein the compression spring (60) has an outer diameter approximately equal to an inner diameter of the hydraulic cylinder (26). 5. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 1 wherein: an underside of the first end cap has a first circular slot (40) surrounding an opening through the first end cap (22) and an underside of the second end cap (24) has a second circular slot (42); and the first circular slot receives the compression end (26a) of the hydraulic cylinder (26) and the second circular slot receives the rebound end (26b) of the hydraulic cylinder. 6. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 1 wherein the piston (44) has a piston wear ring (48) fitted into a first groove (50) formed about a cylindrical outer surface (44a) of the piston (44) to provide a side load bearing area to prevent scoring of an inner surface (27) of the hydraulic cylinder (26). 7. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 6 wherein a piston seal (52) is fitted into a second groove (54) formed in the cylindrical outer surface (44a) of the piston (44) to provide a positive seal with minimal friction to extend seal operation under high pressure. 8. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 1 wherein the plug (62) has an outer cylindrical element (66) which has a diameter that is approximately equal to the inner diameter of the hydraulic cylinder (26). 9. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 8 wherein an outer surface (66a) of the plug (62) rests against the inner surface (22a) of the first end cap (22). 10. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 8 wherein the plug (62) has an inner cylindrical surface element (62a) which intersects the inner facing surface of the outer cylindrical element (66) and has a diameter that is approximately equal to the inner diameter of the compression spring (60) so that the inner cylindrical surface of the plug 

 can be inserted into a first end (60a) of the spring (60). 11. A torque impact mitigator (10) to be used in conjunction with a power tong on an oil drilling rig, comprising: the torque impact mitigator (10) is anchored to the drilling rig, whereby a cable is connected at one end to one end of the torque impact mitigator, and on an opposite second end to the power tong; a second end of the torque impact mitigator is be mounted to a support on the oil well rig. a housing assembly (20) including a hydraulic cylinder (26) having a compression end (26a) and a rebound end (26b); a piston (44) disposed within the hydraulic cylinder (26) so as to reciprocate back and forth; a piston rod (28) disposed within the hydraulic cylinder (26) and mounted at a first end to the piston and having a second end extending out from the compression end; a compression spring (60) disposed between the piston (44) and the compression end (22a) of the hydraulic cylinder (26); a rod clevis (90) secured to the second end of the piston rod (28) to compress the compression spring (60) into a compressed state when the rod clevis is moved away from the hydraulic cylinder and to release the compression spring into a relaxed state when the rod clevis is moved towards the hydraulic cylinder; a plug (62) is disposed within an upper end (60a) of the compression spring and having a bore (64) extending therethrough to receive the piston rod; and one or more bores (56) disposed through the piston to allow the passage of hydraulic fluid into the compression end of the hydraulic cylinder when the compression spring is in the compressed state, and to allow the passage of hydraulic fluid into the rebound end of the hydraulic cylinder when the compression spring is in the relaxed state. 12. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 11 wherein the housing assembly (20) includes first and second end caps (22, 24), wherein the compression end (26a) of the hydraulic cylinder (26) is secured to the first end cap and the rebound end (26b) of the 

 hydraulic cylinder is secured to the second end cap. 13. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 11 wherein the compression spring (60) is disposed about the piston rod (28) and against the piston (44) and against the plug (62). 14. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 13 wherein the compression spring (60) has an outer diameter approximately equal to an inner diameter of the hydraulic cylinder (26). 15. The torque impact mitigator of claim 11 wherein: an underside of the first end cap has a first circular slot (40) surrounding an opening through the first end cap (22) and an underside of the second end cap (24) has a second circular slot (42); and the first circular slot receives the compression end (26a) of the hydraulic cylinder (26) and the second circular slot receives the rebound end (26b) of the hydraulic cylinder. 16. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 11 wherein the piston (44) has a piston wear ring (48) fitted into a first groove (50) formed about a cylindrical outer surface (44a) of the piston (44) to provide a side load bearing area to prevent scoring of an inner surface (27) of the hydraulic cylinder (26). 17. The torque impact mitigator(lO) of claim 16 wherein a piston seal (52) is fitted into a second groove (54) formed in the cylindrical outer surface (44a) of the piston (44) to provide a positive seal with minimal friction to extend seal operation under high pressure. 18. The torque impact mitigator of claim 11 wherein the plug (62) has an outer cylindrical element (66) which has a diameter that is approximately equal to the inner diameter of the hydraulic cylinder (26). 19. The torque impact mitigator (10) of claim 18 wherein an outer surface (66a) of the plug (62) rests against the inner surface (22a) of the first end cap (22). 20. A method of using a torque impact mitigator in conjunction with a power tong on an oil drilling rig, comprising: anchoring the torque impact mitigator to the drilling rig with a cable connected at one end a first end of the torque impact mitigator and on an opposite second end to the power tong; mounting a second end of the torque impact mitigator to a support on the oil well rig; constructing the torque impact mitigator from a housing assembly including a hydraulic cylinder with a compression end and a rebound end; disposing a piston within the hydraulic cylinder so as to reciprocate back and forth; mounting a piston rod at a first end to the piston and extending a second end of the piston rod out from the compression end of the hydraulic cylinder; compressing the compression spring between the piston and the compression end of the hydraulic cylinder into a compressed state when the piston is moved away from the compression end of the hydraulic cylinder and releasing the compression spring into a relaxed state when the piston is moved towards the rebound end of the hydraulic cylinder; reducing the buckling of the compression spring from as the spring is compressed with a plug having a bore extending therethrough disposed within an upper end of the compression spring; and causing the passage of hydraulic fluid through one or more bores in the piston into the compression end of the hydraulic cylinder when the compression spring is in the compressed state and causing the passage of hydraulic fluid through the one or more bores in the piston into the rebound end of the hydraulic cylinder when the compression spring is in the relaxed state.
